lens_requirements.question: How could a rushed researcher, malicious source, overconfident paper, or future maintainer misuse a convenience path or reinterpret a result?

requirements.completion_rule: Do not close on compilation, a single test, or prose. Close only when the normative requirement, all applicable evidence tiers, twenty-lens review, migration, docs, and independent review pass. Otherwise remove/narrow the claim or leave the task OPEN.
requirements.head_mismatch_rule: STOP before editing if HEAD, submodule identity, generated inputs, or relevant upstream release identity changed; produce a diff and re-open affected requirements.
mandatory_adversarial_questions: What invalid or misleading state can still be represented through JSON, Rust API, Python API, CLI, fixture, migration, or manual file edit?
What happens at empty, minimum, maximum, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future-dated, censored, partially initialized, and shutdown boundaries?
Can a failed prerequisite, absent optional component, missing label, abstention, parse error, timeout, or exhausted capacity become a positive result?
Can tuning, visualization, derived artifacts, model selection, or off-repository access leak holdout information?
Can a green synthetic or in-process test pass while the real scientific, security, or deployment claim is false?
Could a reviewer reasonably confuse this output with evidence for H1, H2, H3, H4, EC1, causal use, safety, or PID validity?
requirements.phase_title: Security, privacy, resource, parser, concurrency, and supply-chain hardening
preconditions: Exact clean Prisoma commit and pid-rs submodule identity are recorded.
Every relevant tracked file and generated source is listed in the assignment.
Current behavior and public claim tier are documented before code changes.
Ambiguities and counterexamples are written before selecting semantics.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line and update FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv with blob and SHA-256 identities.
Map each input, state, time, identity, output, failure, resource limit, assumption, and public statement.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable identifiers.
Construct simple, odd, boundary, malformed, stale, replayed, missing, shifted, and adversarial counterexamples.
Implement the smallest coherent change; delete or quarantine contradictory fallback and historical paths.
Add unit, boundary, negative, property/metamorphic, fuzz, state-machine, statistical, and integration tests appropriate to the claim.
Generate evidence tied to exact code, tools, configuration, seeds, hardware, datasets, models, splits, and raw outputs.
Update schemas, migration, docs, claim registry, capability matrix, and exact dependency identities.
Run the local subsystem gate, complete wave gate, and every affected cross-repository conformance check.
Obtain independent review; record residual assumptions and claim impact.
required_evidence: complete commands, logs, and exit statuses
raw and summarized metrics with uncertainty where relevant
positive and negative fixtures or vectors
required_tests: property, metamorphic, or model-based case where applicable
full regression and documentation example gate
requirements.title: Reject duplicate JSON keys, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, overlong strings, deep nesting, and unknown required semantics.

lens_requirements.name: Claims and scope
lens_requirements.question: Could a reasonable reader infer a stronger scientific, engineering, security, or deployment claim than the retained evidence supports?

lens_requirements.name: First-principles semantics
lens_requirements.question: Are every estimand, event, intervention, diagnostic, outcome, state, transition, and refusal defined operationally rather than by suggestive names?

lens_requirements.name: Causal identification
lens_requirements.question: Are treatment assignment, pre-treatment timing, consistency, positivity, interference, confounding, censoring, and estimand identification assumptions explicit and defensible?

lens_requirements.name: Statistics and uncertainty
lens_requirements.question: Are sampling units, dependence, multiplicity, calibration, confidence intervals, missingness, power, stopping, and sensitivity analyses correct?

lens_requirements.name: PID and information theory
lens_requirements.question: Are population, measure, estimator, and application gates separate; are atoms, units, preprocessing, support, and refusal semantics scientifically valid?

lens_requirements.name: Type and state integrity
lens_requirements.question: Can invalid study, split, episode, run-log, capture, intervention, model, or protocol states be represented or silently promoted?

lens_requirements.name: Time, ordering, and replay
lens_requirements.question: Are source time, receipt time, landmark time, treatment time, outcome time, censoring time, epoch, sequence, restart, and replay semantics unambiguous?

lens_requirements.name: Identity and provenance
lens_requirements.question: Can every result be traced to exact code, dependency, model, checkpoint, hook, tensor, dataset, split, transform, seed, configuration, and source artifact?

lens_requirements.name: Data rights, privacy, and ethics
lens_requirements.question: Are licenses, consent, personal data, human-subject issues, dataset rights, redaction, retention, and redistribution addressed before capture or release?

lens_requirements.name: Security and hostile inputs
lens_requirements.question: Can malformed JSON/NPZ/run logs, paths, bridges, NCP frames, model artifacts, or authenticated malicious peers corrupt state, escape confinement, or cause unsafe behavior?

lens_requirements.name: Resource bounds and denial of service
lens_requirements.question: Are bytes, rows, dimensions, events, episodes, artifacts, queues, requests, retries, logs, memory, CPU, disk, and session lifetime bounded before expensive work?

lens_requirements.name: Concurrency, lifecycle, and crash consistency
lens_requirements.question: Are append/ack ordering, partial writes, fsync scope, cancellation, shutdown, retries, restart, poisoned state, and cross-file transactions specified and tested?

lens_requirements.name: Determinism and reproducibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a clean party reproduce builds, generated governance files, fixtures, randomization, simulations, metrics, reports, and package bytes?

lens_requirements.name: API, schema, and SemVer
lens_requirements.question: Is the stable surface minimal and versioned; are unknown fields, migration, panic, allocation, feature, and compatibility rules explicit?

lens_requirements.name: Baselines and falsification
lens_requirements.question: Are strong non-PID comparators, negative controls, placebo checks, shuffled controls, odd/quirky cases, and simpler explanations tested fairly?

lens_requirements.name: Holdout and contamination control
lens_requirements.question: Are tuning and evaluation separated; is access auditable; are transforms fitted train-only; are task/model/family leakage and off-repo access considered?

lens_requirements.name: Verification and evidence quality
lens_requirements.question: Does each claim have the right evidence tier: unit/property/model, independent oracle, formal proof, blind holdout, external benchmark, or real study?

lens_requirements.name: Ecosystem composition
lens_requirements.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, SAFE, Rerun, ROS, and external tools integrated without hidden coupling or authority confusion?

lens_requirements.name: Human factors and thesis defensibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a reviewer understand the theory, assumptions, negative results, status, commands, evidence, and limits without being a Rust expert?

lens_requirements.name: Counterfactual misuse and future maintenance
lens_requirements.question: How could a rushed researcher, malicious source, overconfident paper, or future maintainer misuse a convenience path or reinterpret a result?

lens_requirements.question: Can invalid study, split, episode, run-log, capture, intervention, model, or protocol states be represented or silently promoted?

lens_requirements.name: Time, ordering, and replay
lens_requirements.question: Are source time, receipt time, landmark time, treatment time, outcome time, censoring time, epoch, sequence, restart, and replay semantics unambiguous?

lens_requirements.name: Identity and provenance
lens_requirements.question: Can every result be traced to exact code, dependency, model, checkpoint, hook, tensor, dataset, split, transform, seed, configuration, and source artifact?

lens_requirements.name: Data rights, privacy, and ethics
lens_requirements.question: Are licenses, consent, personal data, human-subject issues, dataset rights, redaction, retention, and redistribution addressed before capture or release?

lens_requirements.name: Security and hostile inputs
lens_requirements.question: Can malformed JSON/NPZ/run logs, paths, bridges, NCP frames, model artifacts, or authenticated malicious peers corrupt state, escape confinement, or cause unsafe behavior?

lens_requirements.name: Resource bounds and denial of service
lens_requirements.question: Are bytes, rows, dimensions, events, episodes, artifacts, queues, requests, retries, logs, memory, CPU, disk, and session lifetime bounded before expensive work?

lens_requirements.name: Concurrency, lifecycle, and crash consistency
lens_requirements.question: Are append/ack ordering, partial writes, fsync scope, cancellation, shutdown, retries, restart, poisoned state, and cross-file transactions specified and tested?

lens_requirements.name: Determinism and reproducibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a clean party reproduce builds, generated governance files, fixtures, randomization, simulations, metrics, reports, and package bytes?

lens_requirements.name: API, schema, and SemVer
lens_requirements.question: Is the stable surface minimal and versioned; are unknown fields, migration, panic, allocation, feature, and compatibility rules explicit?

lens_requirements.name: Baselines and falsification
lens_requirements.question: Are strong non-PID comparators, negative controls, placebo checks, shuffled controls, odd/quirky cases, and simpler explanations tested fairly?

lens_requirements.name: Holdout and contamination control
lens_requirements.question: Are tuning and evaluation separated; is access auditable; are transforms fitted train-only; are task/model/family leakage and off-repo access considered?

lens_requirements.name: Verification and evidence quality
lens_requirements.question: Does each claim have the right evidence tier: unit/property/model, independent oracle, formal proof, blind holdout, external benchmark, or real study?

lens_requirements.name: Ecosystem composition
lens_requirements.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, SAFE, Rerun, ROS, and external tools integrated without hidden coupling or authority confusion?

lens_requirements.name: Human factors and thesis defensibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a reviewer understand the theory, assumptions, negative results, status, commands, evidence, and limits without being a Rust expert?

lens_requirements.name: Counterfactual misuse and future maintenance
lens_requirements.question: How could a rushed researcher, malicious source, overconfident paper, or future maintainer misuse a convenience path or reinterpret a result?

requirements.completion_rule: Do not close on compilation, a single test, or prose. Close only when the normative requirement, all applicable evidence tiers, twenty-lens review, migration, docs, and independent review pass. Otherwise remove/narrow the claim or leave the task OPEN.
requirements.head_mismatch_rule: STOP before editing if HEAD, submodule identity, generated inputs, or relevant upstream release identity changed; produce a diff and re-open affected requirements.
mandatory_adversarial_questions: What invalid or misleading state can still be represented through JSON, Rust API, Python API, CLI, fixture, migration, or manual file edit?
What happens at empty, minimum, maximum, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future-dated, censored, partially initialized, and shutdown boundaries?
Can a failed prerequisite, absent optional component, missing label, abstention, parse error, timeout, or exhausted capacity become a positive result?
Can tuning, visualization, derived artifacts, model selection, or off-repository access leak holdout information?
Can a green synthetic or in-process test pass while the real scientific, security, or deployment claim is false?
Could a reviewer reasonably confuse this output with evidence for H1, H2, H3, H4, EC1, causal use, safety, or PID validity?
requirements.phase_title: Security, privacy, resource, parser, concurrency, and supply-chain hardening
preconditions: Exact clean Prisoma commit and pid-rs submodule identity are recorded.
Every relevant tracked file and generated source is listed in the assignment.
Current behavior and public claim tier are documented before code changes.
Ambiguities and counterexamples are written before selecting semantics.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line and update FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv with blob and SHA-256 identities.
Map each input, state, time, identity, output, failure, resource limit, assumption, and public statement.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable identifiers.
Construct simple, odd, boundary, malformed, stale, replayed, missing, shifted, and adversarial counterexamples.
Implement the smallest coherent change; delete or quarantine contradictory fallback and historical paths.
Add unit, boundary, negative, property/metamorphic, fuzz, state-machine, statistical, and integration tests appropriate to the claim.
Generate evidence tied to exact code, tools, configuration, seeds, hardware, datasets, models, splits, and raw outputs.
Update schemas, migration, docs, claim registry, capability matrix, and exact dependency identities.
Run the local subsystem gate, complete wave gate, and every affected cross-repository conformance check.
Obtain independent review; record residual assumptions and claim impact.
required_evidence: complete commands, logs, and exit statuses
raw and summarized metrics with uncertainty where relevant
positive and negative fixtures or vectors
required_tests: property, metamorphic, or model-based case where applicable
full regression and documentation example gate

Can a failed prerequisite, absent optional component, missing label, abstention, parse error, timeout, or exhausted capacity become a positive result?
Can tuning, visualization, derived artifacts, model selection, or off-repository access leak holdout information?
Can a green synthetic or in-process test pass while the real scientific, security, or deployment claim is false?
Could a reviewer reasonably confuse this output with evidence for H1, H2, H3, H4, EC1, causal use, safety, or PID validity?
requirements.phase_title: Security, privacy, resource, parser, concurrency, and supply-chain hardening
preconditions: Exact clean Prisoma commit and pid-rs submodule identity are recorded.
Every relevant tracked file and generated source is listed in the assignment.
Current behavior and public claim tier are documented before code changes.
Ambiguities and counterexamples are written before selecting semantics.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line and update FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv with blob and SHA-256 identities.
Map each input, state, time, identity, output, failure, resource limit, assumption, and public statement.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable identifiers.
Construct simple, odd, boundary, malformed, stale, replayed, missing, shifted, and adversarial counterexamples.
Implement the smallest coherent change; delete or quarantine contradictory fallback and historical paths.
Add unit, boundary, negative, property/metamorphic, fuzz, state-machine, statistical, and integration tests appropriate to the claim.
Generate evidence tied to exact code, tools, configuration, seeds, hardware, datasets, models, splits, and raw outputs.
Update schemas, migration, docs, claim registry, capability matrix, and exact dependency identities.
Run the local subsystem gate, complete wave gate, and every affected cross-repository conformance check.
Obtain independent review; record residual assumptions and claim impact.
required_evidence: complete commands, logs, and exit statuses
raw and summarized metrics with uncertainty where relevant
positive and negative fixtures or vectors
required_tests: property, metamorphic, or model-based case where applicable
full regression and documentation example gate
requirements.title: Generate SBOM, dependency/license reports, vulnerability scans, and build provenance.

lens_requirements.name: Claims and scope
lens_requirements.question: Could a reasonable reader infer a stronger scientific, engineering, security, or deployment claim than the retained evidence supports?

lens_requirements.name: First-principles semantics
lens_requirements.question: Are every estimand, event, intervention, diagnostic, outcome, state, transition, and refusal defined operationally rather than by suggestive names?

lens_requirements.name: Causal identification
lens_requirements.question: Are treatment assignment, pre-treatment timing, consistency, positivity, interference, confounding, censoring, and estimand identification assumptions explicit and defensible?

lens_requirements.name: Statistics and uncertainty
lens_requirements.question: Are sampling units, dependence, multiplicity, calibration, confidence intervals, missingness, power, stopping, and sensitivity analyses correct?

lens_requirements.name: PID and information theory
lens_requirements.question: Are population, measure, estimator, and application gates separate; are atoms, units, preprocessing, support, and refusal semantics scientifically valid?

lens_requirements.name: Type and state integrity
lens_requirements.question: Can invalid study, split, episode, run-log, capture, intervention, model, or protocol states be represented or silently promoted?

lens_requirements.name: Time, ordering, and replay
lens_requirements.question: Are source time, receipt time, landmark time, treatment time, outcome time, censoring time, epoch, sequence, restart, and replay semantics unambiguous?

lens_requirements.name: Identity and provenance
lens_requirements.question: Can every result be traced to exact code, dependency, model, checkpoint, hook, tensor, dataset, split, transform, seed, configuration, and source artifact?

lens_requirements.name: Data rights, privacy, and ethics
lens_requirements.question: Are licenses, consent, personal data, human-subject issues, dataset rights, redaction, retention, and redistribution addressed before capture or release?

lens_requirements.name: Security and hostile inputs
lens_requirements.question: Can malformed JSON/NPZ/run logs, paths, bridges, NCP frames, model artifacts, or authenticated malicious peers corrupt state, escape confinement, or cause unsafe behavior?

lens_requirements.name: Resource bounds and denial of service
lens_requirements.question: Are bytes, rows, dimensions, events, episodes, artifacts, queues, requests, retries, logs, memory, CPU, disk, and session lifetime bounded before expensive work?

lens_requirements.name: Concurrency, lifecycle, and crash consistency
lens_requirements.question: Are append/ack ordering, partial writes, fsync scope, cancellation, shutdown, retries, restart, poisoned state, and cross-file transactions specified and tested?

lens_requirements.name: Determinism and reproducibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a clean party reproduce builds, generated governance files, fixtures, randomization, simulations, metrics, reports, and package bytes?

lens_requirements.name: API, schema, and SemVer
lens_requirements.question: Is the stable surface minimal and versioned; are unknown fields, migration, panic, allocation, feature, and compatibility rules explicit?

lens_requirements.name: Baselines and falsification
lens_requirements.question: Are strong non-PID comparators, negative controls, placebo checks, shuffled controls, odd/quirky cases, and simpler explanations tested fairly?

lens_requirements.name: Holdout and contamination control
lens_requirements.question: Are tuning and evaluation separated; is access auditable; are transforms fitted train-only; are task/model/family leakage and off-repo access considered?

lens_requirements.name: Verification and evidence quality
lens_requirements.question: Does each claim have the right evidence tier: unit/property/model, independent oracle, formal proof, blind holdout, external benchmark, or real study?

lens_requirements.name: Ecosystem composition
lens_requirements.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, SAFE, Rerun, ROS, and external tools integrated without hidden coupling or authority confusion?

lens_requirements.name: Human factors and thesis defensibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a reviewer understand the theory, assumptions, negative results, status, commands, evidence, and limits without being a Rust expert?

lens_requirements.name: Counterfactual misuse and future maintenance
lens_requirements.question: How could a rushed researcher, malicious source, overconfident paper, or future maintainer misuse a convenience path or reinterpret a result?

requirements.completion_rule: Do not close on compilation, a single test, or prose. Close only when the normative requirement, all applicable evidence tiers, twenty-lens review, migration, docs, and independent review pass. Otherwise remove/narrow the claim or leave the task OPEN.
requirements.head_mismatch_rule: STOP before editing if HEAD, submodule identity, generated inputs, or relevant upstream release identity changed; produce a diff and re-open affected requirements.

lens_requirements.name: Security and hostile inputs
lens_requirements.question: Can malformed JSON/NPZ/run logs, paths, bridges, NCP frames, model artifacts, or authenticated malicious peers corrupt state, escape confinement, or cause unsafe behavior?

lens_requirements.name: Resource bounds and denial of service
lens_requirements.question: Are bytes, rows, dimensions, events, episodes, artifacts, queues, requests, retries, logs, memory, CPU, disk, and session lifetime bounded before expensive work?

lens_requirements.name: Concurrency, lifecycle, and crash consistency
lens_requirements.question: Are append/ack ordering, partial writes, fsync scope, cancellation, shutdown, retries, restart, poisoned state, and cross-file transactions specified and tested?

lens_requirements.name: Determinism and reproducibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a clean party reproduce builds, generated governance files, fixtures, randomization, simulations, metrics, reports, and package bytes?

lens_requirements.name: API, schema, and SemVer
lens_requirements.question: Is the stable surface minimal and versioned; are unknown fields, migration, panic, allocation, feature, and compatibility rules explicit?

lens_requirements.name: Baselines and falsification
lens_requirements.question: Are strong non-PID comparators, negative controls, placebo checks, shuffled controls, odd/quirky cases, and simpler explanations tested fairly?

lens_requirements.name: Holdout and contamination control
lens_requirements.question: Are tuning and evaluation separated; is access auditable; are transforms fitted train-only; are task/model/family leakage and off-repo access considered?

lens_requirements.name: Verification and evidence quality
lens_requirements.question: Does each claim have the right evidence tier: unit/property/model, independent oracle, formal proof, blind holdout, external benchmark, or real study?

lens_requirements.name: Ecosystem composition
lens_requirements.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, SAFE, Rerun, ROS, and external tools integrated without hidden coupling or authority confusion?

lens_requirements.name: Human factors and thesis defensibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a reviewer understand the theory, assumptions, negative results, status, commands, evidence, and limits without being a Rust expert?

lens_requirements.name: Counterfactual misuse and future maintenance
lens_requirements.question: How could a rushed researcher, malicious source, overconfident paper, or future maintainer misuse a convenience path or reinterpret a result?

requirements.completion_rule: Do not close on compilation, a single test, or prose. Close only when the normative requirement, all applicable evidence tiers, twenty-lens review, migration, docs, and independent review pass. Otherwise remove/narrow the claim or leave the task OPEN.
requirements.head_mismatch_rule: STOP before editing if HEAD, submodule identity, generated inputs, or relevant upstream release identity changed; produce a diff and re-open affected requirements.
mandatory_adversarial_questions: What invalid or misleading state can still be represented through JSON, Rust API, Python API, CLI, fixture, migration, or manual file edit?
What happens at empty, minimum, maximum, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future-dated, censored, partially initialized, and shutdown boundaries?
Can a failed prerequisite, absent optional component, missing label, abstention, parse error, timeout, or exhausted capacity become a positive result?
Can tuning, visualization, derived artifacts, model selection, or off-repository access leak holdout information?
Can a green synthetic or in-process test pass while the real scientific, security, or deployment claim is false?
Could a reviewer reasonably confuse this output with evidence for H1, H2, H3, H4, EC1, causal use, safety, or PID validity?
requirements.phase_title: Packaging, CI, reproducibility, usability, and release engineering
preconditions: Exact clean Prisoma commit and pid-rs submodule identity are recorded.
Every relevant tracked file and generated source is listed in the assignment.
Current behavior and public claim tier are documented before code changes.
Ambiguities and counterexamples are written before selecting semantics.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line and update FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv with blob and SHA-256 identities.
Map each input, state, time, identity, output, failure, resource limit, assumption, and public statement.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable identifiers.
Construct simple, odd, boundary, malformed, stale, replayed, missing, shifted, and adversarial counterexamples.
Implement the smallest coherent change; delete or quarantine contradictory fallback and historical paths.
Add unit, boundary, negative, property/metamorphic, fuzz, state-machine, statistical, and integration tests appropriate to the claim.
Generate evidence tied to exact code, tools, configuration, seeds, hardware, datasets, models, splits, and raw outputs.
Update schemas, migration, docs, claim registry, capability matrix, and exact dependency identities.
Run the local subsystem gate, complete wave gate, and every affected cross-repository conformance check.
Obtain independent review; record residual assumptions and claim impact.
required_evidence: complete commands, logs, and exit statuses
raw and summarized metrics with uncertainty where relevant
positive and negative fixtures or vectors
required_tests: property, metamorphic, or model-based case where applicable
full regression and documentation example gate
requirements.title: Decide whether Prisoma publishes a Python package, Rust crates, binaries, a research bundle, or only source, and prevent metadata ambiguity.

lens_requirements.name: Claims and scope
lens_requirements.question: Could a reasonable reader infer a stronger scientific, engineering, security, or deployment claim than the retained evidence supports?

lens_requirements.name: First-principles semantics
lens_requirements.question: Are every estimand, event, intervention, diagnostic, outcome, state, transition, and refusal defined operationally rather than by suggestive names?

lens_requirements.name: Causal identification
lens_requirements.question: Are treatment assignment, pre-treatment timing, consistency, positivity, interference, confounding, censoring, and estimand identification assumptions explicit and defensible?

lens_requirements.name: Statistics and uncertainty
lens_requirements.question: Are sampling units, dependence, multiplicity, calibration, confidence intervals, missingness, power, stopping, and sensitivity analyses correct?

lens_requirements.name: PID and information theory
lens_requirements.question: Are population, measure, estimator, and application gates separate; are atoms, units, preprocessing, support, and refusal semantics scientifically valid?

lens_requirements.name: Type and state integrity
lens_requirements.question: Can invalid study, split, episode, run-log, capture, intervention, model, or protocol states be represented or silently promoted?

lens_requirements.name: Time, ordering, and replay
lens_requirements.question: Are source time, receipt time, landmark time, treatment time, outcome time, censoring time, epoch, sequence, restart, and replay semantics unambiguous?

lens_requirements.name: Identity and provenance
lens_requirements.question: Can every result be traced to exact code, dependency, model, checkpoint, hook, tensor, dataset, split, transform, seed, configuration, and source artifact?

Map each input, state, time, identity, output, failure, resource limit, assumption, and public statement.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable identifiers.
Construct simple, odd, boundary, malformed, stale, replayed, missing, shifted, and adversarial counterexamples.
Implement the smallest coherent change; delete or quarantine contradictory fallback and historical paths.
Add unit, boundary, negative, property/metamorphic, fuzz, state-machine, statistical, and integration tests appropriate to the claim.
Generate evidence tied to exact code, tools, configuration, seeds, hardware, datasets, models, splits, and raw outputs.
Update schemas, migration, docs, claim registry, capability matrix, and exact dependency identities.
Run the local subsystem gate, complete wave gate, and every affected cross-repository conformance check.
Obtain independent review; record residual assumptions and claim impact.
required_evidence: complete commands, logs, and exit statuses
raw and summarized metrics with uncertainty where relevant
positive and negative fixtures or vectors
required_tests: property, metamorphic, or model-based case where applicable
full regression and documentation example gate

lens_requirements.name: Claims and scope
lens_requirements.question: Could a reasonable reader infer a stronger scientific, engineering, security, or deployment claim than the retained evidence supports?

lens_requirements.name: First-principles semantics
lens_requirements.question: Are every estimand, event, intervention, diagnostic, outcome, state, transition, and refusal defined operationally rather than by suggestive names?

lens_requirements.name: Causal identification
lens_requirements.question: Are treatment assignment, pre-treatment timing, consistency, positivity, interference, confounding, censoring, and estimand identification assumptions explicit and defensible?

lens_requirements.name: Statistics and uncertainty
lens_requirements.question: Are sampling units, dependence, multiplicity, calibration, confidence intervals, missingness, power, stopping, and sensitivity analyses correct?

lens_requirements.name: PID and information theory
lens_requirements.question: Are population, measure, estimator, and application gates separate; are atoms, units, preprocessing, support, and refusal semantics scientifically valid?

lens_requirements.name: Type and state integrity
lens_requirements.question: Can invalid study, split, episode, run-log, capture, intervention, model, or protocol states be represented or silently promoted?

lens_requirements.name: Time, ordering, and replay
lens_requirements.question: Are source time, receipt time, landmark time, treatment time, outcome time, censoring time, epoch, sequence, restart, and replay semantics unambiguous?

lens_requirements.name: Identity and provenance
lens_requirements.question: Can every result be traced to exact code, dependency, model, checkpoint, hook, tensor, dataset, split, transform, seed, configuration, and source artifact?

lens_requirements.name: Data rights, privacy, and ethics
lens_requirements.question: Are licenses, consent, personal data, human-subject issues, dataset rights, redaction, retention, and redistribution addressed before capture or release?

lens_requirements.name: Security and hostile inputs
lens_requirements.question: Can malformed JSON/NPZ/run logs, paths, bridges, NCP frames, model artifacts, or authenticated malicious peers corrupt state, escape confinement, or cause unsafe behavior?

lens_requirements.name: Resource bounds and denial of service
lens_requirements.question: Are bytes, rows, dimensions, events, episodes, artifacts, queues, requests, retries, logs, memory, CPU, disk, and session lifetime bounded before expensive work?

lens_requirements.name: Concurrency, lifecycle, and crash consistency
lens_requirements.question: Are append/ack ordering, partial writes, fsync scope, cancellation, shutdown, retries, restart, poisoned state, and cross-file transactions specified and tested?

lens_requirements.name: Determinism and reproducibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a clean party reproduce builds, generated governance files, fixtures, randomization, simulations, metrics, reports, and package bytes?

lens_requirements.name: API, schema, and SemVer
lens_requirements.question: Is the stable surface minimal and versioned; are unknown fields, migration, panic, allocation, feature, and compatibility rules explicit?

lens_requirements.name: Baselines and falsification
lens_requirements.question: Are strong non-PID comparators, negative controls, placebo checks, shuffled controls, odd/quirky cases, and simpler explanations tested fairly?

lens_requirements.name: Holdout and contamination control
lens_requirements.question: Are tuning and evaluation separated; is access auditable; are transforms fitted train-only; are task/model/family leakage and off-repo access considered?

lens_requirements.name: Verification and evidence quality
lens_requirements.question: Does each claim have the right evidence tier: unit/property/model, independent oracle, formal proof, blind holdout, external benchmark, or real study?

lens_requirements.name: Ecosystem composition
lens_requirements.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, SAFE, Rerun, ROS, and external tools integrated without hidden coupling or authority confusion?

lens_requirements.name: Human factors and thesis defensibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a reviewer understand the theory, assumptions, negative results, status, commands, evidence, and limits without being a Rust expert?

lens_requirements.name: Counterfactual misuse and future maintenance
lens_requirements.question: How could a rushed researcher, malicious source, overconfident paper, or future maintainer misuse a convenience path or reinterpret a result?

requirements.completion_rule: Do not close on compilation, a single test, or prose. Close only when the normative requirement, all applicable evidence tiers, twenty-lens review, migration, docs, and independent review pass. Otherwise remove/narrow the claim or leave the task OPEN.
requirements.head_mismatch_rule: STOP before editing if HEAD, submodule identity, generated inputs, or relevant upstream release identity changed; produce a diff and re-open affected requirements.
mandatory_adversarial_questions: What invalid or misleading state can still be represented through JSON, Rust API, Python API, CLI, fixture, migration, or manual file edit?
What happens at empty, minimum, maximum, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future-dated, censored, partially initialized, and shutdown boundaries?
Can a failed prerequisite, absent optional component, missing label, abstention, parse error, timeout, or exhausted capacity become a positive result?
Can tuning, visualization, derived artifacts, model selection, or off-repository access leak holdout information?
Can a green synthetic or in-process test pass while the real scientific, security, or deployment claim is false?
Could a reviewer reasonably confuse this output with evidence for H1, H2, H3, H4, EC1, causal use, safety, or PID validity?

lens_requirements.question: Are strong non-PID comparators, negative controls, placebo checks, shuffled controls, odd/quirky cases, and simpler explanations tested fairly?

lens_requirements.name: Holdout and contamination control
lens_requirements.question: Are tuning and evaluation separated; is access auditable; are transforms fitted train-only; are task/model/family leakage and off-repo access considered?

lens_requirements.name: Verification and evidence quality
lens_requirements.question: Does each claim have the right evidence tier: unit/property/model, independent oracle, formal proof, blind holdout, external benchmark, or real study?

lens_requirements.name: Ecosystem composition
lens_requirements.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, SAFE, Rerun, ROS, and external tools integrated without hidden coupling or authority confusion?

lens_requirements.name: Human factors and thesis defensibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a reviewer understand the theory, assumptions, negative results, status, commands, evidence, and limits without being a Rust expert?

lens_requirements.name: Counterfactual misuse and future maintenance
lens_requirements.question: How could a rushed researcher, malicious source, overconfident paper, or future maintainer misuse a convenience path or reinterpret a result?

requirements.completion_rule: Do not close on compilation, a single test, or prose. Close only when the normative requirement, all applicable evidence tiers, twenty-lens review, migration, docs, and independent review pass. Otherwise remove/narrow the claim or leave the task OPEN.
requirements.head_mismatch_rule: STOP before editing if HEAD, submodule identity, generated inputs, or relevant upstream release identity changed; produce a diff and re-open affected requirements.
mandatory_adversarial_questions: What invalid or misleading state can still be represented through JSON, Rust API, Python API, CLI, fixture, migration, or manual file edit?
What happens at empty, minimum, maximum, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future-dated, censored, partially initialized, and shutdown boundaries?
Can a failed prerequisite, absent optional component, missing label, abstention, parse error, timeout, or exhausted capacity become a positive result?
Can tuning, visualization, derived artifacts, model selection, or off-repository access leak holdout information?
Can a green synthetic or in-process test pass while the real scientific, security, or deployment claim is false?
Could a reviewer reasonably confuse this output with evidence for H1, H2, H3, H4, EC1, causal use, safety, or PID validity?
requirements.phase_title: Packaging, CI, reproducibility, usability, and release engineering
preconditions: Exact clean Prisoma commit and pid-rs submodule identity are recorded.
Every relevant tracked file and generated source is listed in the assignment.
Current behavior and public claim tier are documented before code changes.
Ambiguities and counterexamples are written before selecting semantics.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line and update FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv with blob and SHA-256 identities.
Map each input, state, time, identity, output, failure, resource limit, assumption, and public statement.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable identifiers.
Construct simple, odd, boundary, malformed, stale, replayed, missing, shifted, and adversarial counterexamples.
Implement the smallest coherent change; delete or quarantine contradictory fallback and historical paths.
Add unit, boundary, negative, property/metamorphic, fuzz, state-machine, statistical, and integration tests appropriate to the claim.
Generate evidence tied to exact code, tools, configuration, seeds, hardware, datasets, models, splits, and raw outputs.
Update schemas, migration, docs, claim registry, capability matrix, and exact dependency identities.
Run the local subsystem gate, complete wave gate, and every affected cross-repository conformance check.
Obtain independent review; record residual assumptions and claim impact.
required_evidence: complete commands, logs, and exit statuses
raw and summarized metrics with uncertainty where relevant
positive and negative fixtures or vectors
required_tests: property, metamorphic, or model-based case where applicable
full regression and documentation example gate
requirements.title: Create migration tools for schemas and captures only where conversion is lossless.

lens_requirements.name: Claims and scope
lens_requirements.question: Could a reasonable reader infer a stronger scientific, engineering, security, or deployment claim than the retained evidence supports?

lens_requirements.name: First-principles semantics
lens_requirements.question: Are every estimand, event, intervention, diagnostic, outcome, state, transition, and refusal defined operationally rather than by suggestive names?

lens_requirements.name: Causal identification
lens_requirements.question: Are treatment assignment, pre-treatment timing, consistency, positivity, interference, confounding, censoring, and estimand identification assumptions explicit and defensible?

lens_requirements.name: Statistics and uncertainty
lens_requirements.question: Are sampling units, dependence, multiplicity, calibration, confidence intervals, missingness, power, stopping, and sensitivity analyses correct?

lens_requirements.name: PID and information theory
lens_requirements.question: Are population, measure, estimator, and application gates separate; are atoms, units, preprocessing, support, and refusal semantics scientifically valid?

lens_requirements.name: Type and state integrity
lens_requirements.question: Can invalid study, split, episode, run-log, capture, intervention, model, or protocol states be represented or silently promoted?

lens_requirements.name: Time, ordering, and replay
lens_requirements.question: Are source time, receipt time, landmark time, treatment time, outcome time, censoring time, epoch, sequence, restart, and replay semantics unambiguous?

lens_requirements.name: Identity and provenance
lens_requirements.question: Can every result be traced to exact code, dependency, model, checkpoint, hook, tensor, dataset, split, transform, seed, configuration, and source artifact?

lens_requirements.name: Data rights, privacy, and ethics
lens_requirements.question: Are licenses, consent, personal data, human-subject issues, dataset rights, redaction, retention, and redistribution addressed before capture or release?

lens_requirements.name: Security and hostile inputs
lens_requirements.question: Can malformed JSON/NPZ/run logs, paths, bridges, NCP frames, model artifacts, or authenticated malicious peers corrupt state, escape confinement, or cause unsafe behavior?

lens_requirements.name: Resource bounds and denial of service
lens_requirements.question: Are bytes, rows, dimensions, events, episodes, artifacts, queues, requests, retries, logs, memory, CPU, disk, and session lifetime bounded before expensive work?

lens_requirements.name: Concurrency, lifecycle, and crash consistency
lens_requirements.question: Are append/ack ordering, partial writes, fsync scope, cancellation, shutdown, retries, restart, poisoned state, and cross-file transactions specified and tested?

lens_requirements.name: Determinism and reproducibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a clean party reproduce builds, generated governance files, fixtures, randomization, simulations, metrics, reports, and package bytes?

lens_requirements.name: Claims and scope
lens_requirements.question: Could a reasonable reader infer a stronger scientific, engineering, security, or deployment claim than the retained evidence supports?

lens_requirements.name: First-principles semantics
lens_requirements.question: Are every estimand, event, intervention, diagnostic, outcome, state, transition, and refusal defined operationally rather than by suggestive names?

lens_requirements.name: Causal identification
lens_requirements.question: Are treatment assignment, pre-treatment timing, consistency, positivity, interference, confounding, censoring, and estimand identification assumptions explicit and defensible?

lens_requirements.name: Statistics and uncertainty
lens_requirements.question: Are sampling units, dependence, multiplicity, calibration, confidence intervals, missingness, power, stopping, and sensitivity analyses correct?

lens_requirements.name: PID and information theory
lens_requirements.question: Are population, measure, estimator, and application gates separate; are atoms, units, preprocessing, support, and refusal semantics scientifically valid?

lens_requirements.name: Type and state integrity
lens_requirements.question: Can invalid study, split, episode, run-log, capture, intervention, model, or protocol states be represented or silently promoted?

lens_requirements.name: Time, ordering, and replay
lens_requirements.question: Are source time, receipt time, landmark time, treatment time, outcome time, censoring time, epoch, sequence, restart, and replay semantics unambiguous?

lens_requirements.name: Identity and provenance
lens_requirements.question: Can every result be traced to exact code, dependency, model, checkpoint, hook, tensor, dataset, split, transform, seed, configuration, and source artifact?

lens_requirements.name: Data rights, privacy, and ethics
lens_requirements.question: Are licenses, consent, personal data, human-subject issues, dataset rights, redaction, retention, and redistribution addressed before capture or release?

lens_requirements.name: Security and hostile inputs
lens_requirements.question: Can malformed JSON/NPZ/run logs, paths, bridges, NCP frames, model artifacts, or authenticated malicious peers corrupt state, escape confinement, or cause unsafe behavior?

lens_requirements.name: Resource bounds and denial of service
lens_requirements.question: Are bytes, rows, dimensions, events, episodes, artifacts, queues, requests, retries, logs, memory, CPU, disk, and session lifetime bounded before expensive work?

lens_requirements.name: Concurrency, lifecycle, and crash consistency
lens_requirements.question: Are append/ack ordering, partial writes, fsync scope, cancellation, shutdown, retries, restart, poisoned state, and cross-file transactions specified and tested?

lens_requirements.name: Determinism and reproducibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a clean party reproduce builds, generated governance files, fixtures, randomization, simulations, metrics, reports, and package bytes?

lens_requirements.name: API, schema, and SemVer
lens_requirements.question: Is the stable surface minimal and versioned; are unknown fields, migration, panic, allocation, feature, and compatibility rules explicit?

lens_requirements.name: Baselines and falsification
lens_requirements.question: Are strong non-PID comparators, negative controls, placebo checks, shuffled controls, odd/quirky cases, and simpler explanations tested fairly?

lens_requirements.name: Holdout and contamination control
lens_requirements.question: Are tuning and evaluation separated; is access auditable; are transforms fitted train-only; are task/model/family leakage and off-repo access considered?

lens_requirements.name: Verification and evidence quality
lens_requirements.question: Does each claim have the right evidence tier: unit/property/model, independent oracle, formal proof, blind holdout, external benchmark, or real study?

lens_requirements.name: Ecosystem composition
lens_requirements.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, SAFE, Rerun, ROS, and external tools integrated without hidden coupling or authority confusion?

lens_requirements.name: Human factors and thesis defensibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a reviewer understand the theory, assumptions, negative results, status, commands, evidence, and limits without being a Rust expert?

lens_requirements.name: Counterfactual misuse and future maintenance
lens_requirements.question: How could a rushed researcher, malicious source, overconfident paper, or future maintainer misuse a convenience path or reinterpret a result?

requirements.completion_rule: Do not close on compilation, a single test, or prose. Close only when the normative requirement, all applicable evidence tiers, twenty-lens review, migration, docs, and independent review pass. Otherwise remove/narrow the claim or leave the task OPEN.
requirements.head_mismatch_rule: STOP before editing if HEAD, submodule identity, generated inputs, or relevant upstream release identity changed; produce a diff and re-open affected requirements.
mandatory_adversarial_questions: What invalid or misleading state can still be represented through JSON, Rust API, Python API, CLI, fixture, migration, or manual file edit?
What happens at empty, minimum, maximum, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future-dated, censored, partially initialized, and shutdown boundaries?
Can a failed prerequisite, absent optional component, missing label, abstention, parse error, timeout, or exhausted capacity become a positive result?
Can tuning, visualization, derived artifacts, model selection, or off-repository access leak holdout information?
Can a green synthetic or in-process test pass while the real scientific, security, or deployment claim is false?
Could a reviewer reasonably confuse this output with evidence for H1, H2, H3, H4, EC1, causal use, safety, or PID validity?
requirements.phase_title: Packaging, CI, reproducibility, usability, and release engineering
preconditions: Exact clean Prisoma commit and pid-rs submodule identity are recorded.
Every relevant tracked file and generated source is listed in the assignment.
Current behavior and public claim tier are documented before code changes.
Ambiguities and counterexamples are written before selecting semantics.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line and update FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv with blob and SHA-256 identities.
Map each input, state, time, identity, output, failure, resource limit, assumption, and public statement.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable identifiers.
Construct simple, odd, boundary, malformed, stale, replayed, missing, shifted, and adversarial counterexamples.
Implement the smallest coherent change; delete or quarantine contradictory fallback and historical paths.
Add unit, boundary, negative, property/metamorphic, fuzz, state-machine, statistical, and integration tests appropriate to the claim.
Generate evidence tied to exact code, tools, configuration, seeds, hardware, datasets, models, splits, and raw outputs.
Update schemas, migration, docs, claim registry, capability matrix, and exact dependency identities.
Run the local subsystem gate, complete wave gate, and every affected cross-repository conformance check.
Obtain independent review; record residual assumptions and claim impact.
required_evidence: complete commands, logs, and exit statuses
raw and summarized metrics with uncertainty where relevant
positive and negative fixtures or vectors
required_tests: property, metamorphic, or model-based case where applicable
full regression and documentation example gate

requirements.completion_rule: Do not close on compilation, a single test, or prose. Close only when the normative requirement, all applicable evidence tiers, twenty-lens review, migration, docs, and independent review pass. Otherwise remove/narrow the claim or leave the task OPEN.
requirements.head_mismatch_rule: STOP before editing if HEAD, submodule identity, generated inputs, or relevant upstream release identity changed; produce a diff and re-open affected requirements.
mandatory_adversarial_questions: What invalid or misleading state can still be represented through JSON, Rust API, Python API, CLI, fixture, migration, or manual file edit?
What happens at empty, minimum, maximum, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future-dated, censored, partially initialized, and shutdown boundaries?
Can a failed prerequisite, absent optional component, missing label, abstention, parse error, timeout, or exhausted capacity become a positive result?
Can tuning, visualization, derived artifacts, model selection, or off-repository access leak holdout information?
Can a green synthetic or in-process test pass while the real scientific, security, or deployment claim is false?
Could a reviewer reasonably confuse this output with evidence for H1, H2, H3, H4, EC1, causal use, safety, or PID validity?
requirements.phase_title: Independent verification, thesis-board evidence, local convergence, and final release
preconditions: Exact clean Prisoma commit and pid-rs submodule identity are recorded.
Every relevant tracked file and generated source is listed in the assignment.
Current behavior and public claim tier are documented before code changes.
Ambiguities and counterexamples are written before selecting semantics.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line and update FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv with blob and SHA-256 identities.
Map each input, state, time, identity, output, failure, resource limit, assumption, and public statement.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable identifiers.
Construct simple, odd, boundary, malformed, stale, replayed, missing, shifted, and adversarial counterexamples.
Implement the smallest coherent change; delete or quarantine contradictory fallback and historical paths.
Add unit, boundary, negative, property/metamorphic, fuzz, state-machine, statistical, and integration tests appropriate to the claim.
Generate evidence tied to exact code, tools, configuration, seeds, hardware, datasets, models, splits, and raw outputs.
Update schemas, migration, docs, claim registry, capability matrix, and exact dependency identities.
Run the local subsystem gate, complete wave gate, and every affected cross-repository conformance check.
Obtain independent review; record residual assumptions and claim impact.
required_evidence: complete commands, logs, and exit statuses
raw and summarized metrics with uncertainty where relevant
positive and negative fixtures or vectors
required_tests: property, metamorphic, or model-based case where applicable
full regression and documentation example gate
requirements.title: Commission independent causal/statistical review of M0 and each confirmatory protocol.

lens_requirements.name: Claims and scope
lens_requirements.question: Could a reasonable reader infer a stronger scientific, engineering, security, or deployment claim than the retained evidence supports?

lens_requirements.name: First-principles semantics
lens_requirements.question: Are every estimand, event, intervention, diagnostic, outcome, state, transition, and refusal defined operationally rather than by suggestive names?

lens_requirements.name: Causal identification
lens_requirements.question: Are treatment assignment, pre-treatment timing, consistency, positivity, interference, confounding, censoring, and estimand identification assumptions explicit and defensible?

lens_requirements.name: Statistics and uncertainty
lens_requirements.question: Are sampling units, dependence, multiplicity, calibration, confidence intervals, missingness, power, stopping, and sensitivity analyses correct?

lens_requirements.name: PID and information theory
lens_requirements.question: Are population, measure, estimator, and application gates separate; are atoms, units, preprocessing, support, and refusal semantics scientifically valid?

lens_requirements.name: Type and state integrity
lens_requirements.question: Can invalid study, split, episode, run-log, capture, intervention, model, or protocol states be represented or silently promoted?

lens_requirements.name: Time, ordering, and replay
lens_requirements.question: Are source time, receipt time, landmark time, treatment time, outcome time, censoring time, epoch, sequence, restart, and replay semantics unambiguous?

lens_requirements.name: Identity and provenance
lens_requirements.question: Can every result be traced to exact code, dependency, model, checkpoint, hook, tensor, dataset, split, transform, seed, configuration, and source artifact?

lens_requirements.name: Data rights, privacy, and ethics
lens_requirements.question: Are licenses, consent, personal data, human-subject issues, dataset rights, redaction, retention, and redistribution addressed before capture or release?

lens_requirements.name: Security and hostile inputs
lens_requirements.question: Can malformed JSON/NPZ/run logs, paths, bridges, NCP frames, model artifacts, or authenticated malicious peers corrupt state, escape confinement, or cause unsafe behavior?

lens_requirements.name: Resource bounds and denial of service
lens_requirements.question: Are bytes, rows, dimensions, events, episodes, artifacts, queues, requests, retries, logs, memory, CPU, disk, and session lifetime bounded before expensive work?

lens_requirements.name: Concurrency, lifecycle, and crash consistency
lens_requirements.question: Are append/ack ordering, partial writes, fsync scope, cancellation, shutdown, retries, restart, poisoned state, and cross-file transactions specified and tested?

lens_requirements.name: Determinism and reproducibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a clean party reproduce builds, generated governance files, fixtures, randomization, simulations, metrics, reports, and package bytes?

lens_requirements.name: API, schema, and SemVer
lens_requirements.question: Is the stable surface minimal and versioned; are unknown fields, migration, panic, allocation, feature, and compatibility rules explicit?

lens_requirements.name: Baselines and falsification
lens_requirements.question: Are strong non-PID comparators, negative controls, placebo checks, shuffled controls, odd/quirky cases, and simpler explanations tested fairly?

lens_requirements.name: Holdout and contamination control
lens_requirements.question: Are tuning and evaluation separated; is access auditable; are transforms fitted train-only; are task/model/family leakage and off-repo access considered?

lens_requirements.name: Verification and evidence quality
lens_requirements.question: Does each claim have the right evidence tier: unit/property/model, independent oracle, formal proof, blind holdout, external benchmark, or real study?

lens_requirements.name: Ecosystem composition
lens_requirements.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, SAFE, Rerun, ROS, and external tools integrated without hidden coupling or authority confusion?

lens_requirements.name: Human factors and thesis defensibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a reviewer understand the theory, assumptions, negative results, status, commands, evidence, and limits without being a Rust expert?

lens_requirements.name: Counterfactual misuse and future maintenance
lens_requirements.question: How could a rushed researcher, malicious source, overconfident paper, or future maintainer misuse a convenience path or reinterpret a result?

requirements.head_mismatch_rule: STOP before editing if HEAD, submodule identity, generated inputs, or relevant upstream release identity changed; produce a diff and re-open affected requirements.

lens_requirements.question: Are source time, receipt time, landmark time, treatment time, outcome time, censoring time, epoch, sequence, restart, and replay semantics unambiguous?

lens_requirements.name: Identity and provenance
lens_requirements.question: Can every result be traced to exact code, dependency, model, checkpoint, hook, tensor, dataset, split, transform, seed, configuration, and source artifact?

lens_requirements.name: Data rights, privacy, and ethics
lens_requirements.question: Are licenses, consent, personal data, human-subject issues, dataset rights, redaction, retention, and redistribution addressed before capture or release?

lens_requirements.name: Security and hostile inputs
lens_requirements.question: Can malformed JSON/NPZ/run logs, paths, bridges, NCP frames, model artifacts, or authenticated malicious peers corrupt state, escape confinement, or cause unsafe behavior?

lens_requirements.name: Resource bounds and denial of service
lens_requirements.question: Are bytes, rows, dimensions, events, episodes, artifacts, queues, requests, retries, logs, memory, CPU, disk, and session lifetime bounded before expensive work?

lens_requirements.name: Concurrency, lifecycle, and crash consistency
lens_requirements.question: Are append/ack ordering, partial writes, fsync scope, cancellation, shutdown, retries, restart, poisoned state, and cross-file transactions specified and tested?

lens_requirements.name: Determinism and reproducibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a clean party reproduce builds, generated governance files, fixtures, randomization, simulations, metrics, reports, and package bytes?

lens_requirements.name: API, schema, and SemVer
lens_requirements.question: Is the stable surface minimal and versioned; are unknown fields, migration, panic, allocation, feature, and compatibility rules explicit?

lens_requirements.name: Baselines and falsification
lens_requirements.question: Are strong non-PID comparators, negative controls, placebo checks, shuffled controls, odd/quirky cases, and simpler explanations tested fairly?

lens_requirements.name: Holdout and contamination control
lens_requirements.question: Are tuning and evaluation separated; is access auditable; are transforms fitted train-only; are task/model/family leakage and off-repo access considered?

lens_requirements.name: Verification and evidence quality
lens_requirements.question: Does each claim have the right evidence tier: unit/property/model, independent oracle, formal proof, blind holdout, external benchmark, or real study?

lens_requirements.name: Ecosystem composition
lens_requirements.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, SAFE, Rerun, ROS, and external tools integrated without hidden coupling or authority confusion?

lens_requirements.name: Human factors and thesis defensibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a reviewer understand the theory, assumptions, negative results, status, commands, evidence, and limits without being a Rust expert?

lens_requirements.name: Counterfactual misuse and future maintenance
lens_requirements.question: How could a rushed researcher, malicious source, overconfident paper, or future maintainer misuse a convenience path or reinterpret a result?

requirements.completion_rule: Do not close on compilation, a single test, or prose. Close only when the normative requirement, all applicable evidence tiers, twenty-lens review, migration, docs, and independent review pass. Otherwise remove/narrow the claim or leave the task OPEN.
requirements.head_mismatch_rule: STOP before editing if HEAD, submodule identity, generated inputs, or relevant upstream release identity changed; produce a diff and re-open affected requirements.
mandatory_adversarial_questions: What invalid or misleading state can still be represented through JSON, Rust API, Python API, CLI, fixture, migration, or manual file edit?
What happens at empty, minimum, maximum, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future-dated, censored, partially initialized, and shutdown boundaries?
Can a failed prerequisite, absent optional component, missing label, abstention, parse error, timeout, or exhausted capacity become a positive result?
Can tuning, visualization, derived artifacts, model selection, or off-repository access leak holdout information?
Can a green synthetic or in-process test pass while the real scientific, security, or deployment claim is false?
Could a reviewer reasonably confuse this output with evidence for H1, H2, H3, H4, EC1, causal use, safety, or PID validity?
requirements.phase_title: Independent verification, thesis-board evidence, local convergence, and final release
preconditions: Exact clean Prisoma commit and pid-rs submodule identity are recorded.
Every relevant tracked file and generated source is listed in the assignment.
Current behavior and public claim tier are documented before code changes.
Ambiguities and counterexamples are written before selecting semantics.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line and update FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv with blob and SHA-256 identities.
Map each input, state, time, identity, output, failure, resource limit, assumption, and public statement.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable identifiers.
Construct simple, odd, boundary, malformed, stale, replayed, missing, shifted, and adversarial counterexamples.
Implement the smallest coherent change; delete or quarantine contradictory fallback and historical paths.
Add unit, boundary, negative, property/metamorphic, fuzz, state-machine, statistical, and integration tests appropriate to the claim.
Generate evidence tied to exact code, tools, configuration, seeds, hardware, datasets, models, splits, and raw outputs.
Update schemas, migration, docs, claim registry, capability matrix, and exact dependency identities.
Run the local subsystem gate, complete wave gate, and every affected cross-repository conformance check.
Obtain independent review; record residual assumptions and claim impact.

raw and summarized metrics with uncertainty where relevant
positive and negative fixtures or vectors
required_tests: property, metamorphic, or model-based case where applicable
full regression and documentation example gate

Could a reviewer reasonably confuse this output with evidence for H1, H2, H3, H4, EC1, causal use, safety, or PID validity?

preconditions: Exact clean Prisoma commit and pid-rs submodule identity are recorded.
Every relevant tracked file and generated source is listed in the assignment.
Current behavior and public claim tier are documented before code changes.
Ambiguities and counterexamples are written before selecting semantics.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line and update FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv with blob and SHA-256 identities.
Map each input, state, time, identity, output, failure, resource limit, assumption, and public statement.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable identifiers.
Construct simple, odd, boundary, malformed, stale, replayed, missing, shifted, and adversarial counterexamples.
Implement the smallest coherent change; delete or quarantine contradictory fallback and historical paths.
Add unit, boundary, negative, property/metamorphic, fuzz, state-machine, statistical, and integration tests appropriate to the claim.
Generate evidence tied to exact code, tools, configuration, seeds, hardware, datasets, models, splits, and raw outputs.
Update schemas, migration, docs, claim registry, capability matrix, and exact dependency identities.
Run the local subsystem gate, complete wave gate, and every affected cross-repository conformance check.
Obtain independent review; record residual assumptions and claim impact.

lens_requirements.name: Claims and scope
lens_requirements.question: Could a reasonable reader infer a stronger scientific, engineering, security, or deployment claim than the retained evidence supports?

lens_requirements.name: First-principles semantics
lens_requirements.question: Are every estimand, event, intervention, diagnostic, outcome, state, transition, and refusal defined operationally rather than by suggestive names?

lens_requirements.name: Causal identification
lens_requirements.question: Are treatment assignment, pre-treatment timing, consistency, positivity, interference, confounding, censoring, and estimand identification assumptions explicit and defensible?

lens_requirements.name: Statistics and uncertainty
lens_requirements.question: Are sampling units, dependence, multiplicity, calibration, confidence intervals, missingness, power, stopping, and sensitivity analyses correct?

lens_requirements.name: PID and information theory
lens_requirements.question: Are population, measure, estimator, and application gates separate; are atoms, units, preprocessing, support, and refusal semantics scientifically valid?

lens_requirements.name: Type and state integrity
lens_requirements.question: Can invalid study, split, episode, run-log, capture, intervention, model, or protocol states be represented or silently promoted?

lens_requirements.name: Time, ordering, and replay
lens_requirements.question: Are source time, receipt time, landmark time, treatment time, outcome time, censoring time, epoch, sequence, restart, and replay semantics unambiguous?

lens_requirements.name: Identity and provenance
lens_requirements.question: Can every result be traced to exact code, dependency, model, checkpoint, hook, tensor, dataset, split, transform, seed, configuration, and source artifact?

lens_requirements.name: Data rights, privacy, and ethics
lens_requirements.question: Are licenses, consent, personal data, human-subject issues, dataset rights, redaction, retention, and redistribution addressed before capture or release?

lens_requirements.name: Security and hostile inputs
lens_requirements.question: Can malformed JSON/NPZ/run logs, paths, bridges, NCP frames, model artifacts, or authenticated malicious peers corrupt state, escape confinement, or cause unsafe behavior?

lens_requirements.name: Resource bounds and denial of service
lens_requirements.question: Are bytes, rows, dimensions, events, episodes, artifacts, queues, requests, retries, logs, memory, CPU, disk, and session lifetime bounded before expensive work?

lens_requirements.name: Concurrency, lifecycle, and crash consistency
lens_requirements.question: Are append/ack ordering, partial writes, fsync scope, cancellation, shutdown, retries, restart, poisoned state, and cross-file transactions specified and tested?

lens_requirements.name: Determinism and reproducibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a clean party reproduce builds, generated governance files, fixtures, randomization, simulations, metrics, reports, and package bytes?

lens_requirements.name: API, schema, and SemVer
lens_requirements.question: Is the stable surface minimal and versioned; are unknown fields, migration, panic, allocation, feature, and compatibility rules explicit?

lens_requirements.name: Baselines and falsification
lens_requirements.question: Are strong non-PID comparators, negative controls, placebo checks, shuffled controls, odd/quirky cases, and simpler explanations tested fairly?

lens_requirements.name: Holdout and contamination control
lens_requirements.question: Are tuning and evaluation separated; is access auditable; are transforms fitted train-only; are task/model/family leakage and off-repo access considered?

lens_requirements.name: Verification and evidence quality
lens_requirements.question: Does each claim have the right evidence tier: unit/property/model, independent oracle, formal proof, blind holdout, external benchmark, or real study?

lens_requirements.name: Ecosystem composition
lens_requirements.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, SAFE, Rerun, ROS, and external tools integrated without hidden coupling or authority confusion?

lens_requirements.name: Human factors and thesis defensibility
lens_requirements.question: Can a reviewer understand the theory, assumptions, negative results, status, commands, evidence, and limits without being a Rust expert?

lens_requirements.name: Counterfactual misuse and future maintenance
lens_requirements.question: How could a rushed researcher, malicious source, overconfident paper, or future maintainer misuse a convenience path or reinterpret a result?

requirements.completion_rule: Do not close on compilation, a single test, or prose. Close only when the normative requirement, all applicable evidence tiers, twenty-lens review, migration, docs, and independent review pass. Otherwise remove/narrow the claim or leave the task OPEN.
requirements.head_mismatch_rule: STOP before editing if HEAD, submodule identity, generated inputs, or relevant upstream release identity changed; produce a diff and re-open affected requirements.
mandatory_adversarial_questions: What invalid or misleading state can still be represented through JSON, Rust API, Python API, CLI, fixture, migration, or manual file edit?
What happens at empty, minimum, maximum, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future-dated, censored, partially initialized, and shutdown boundaries?
Can a failed prerequisite, absent optional component, missing label, abstention, parse error, timeout, or exhausted capacity become a positive result?
Can tuning, visualization, derived artifacts, model selection, or off-repository access leak holdout information?
Can a green synthetic or in-process test pass while the real scientific, security, or deployment claim is false?
Could a reviewer reasonably confuse this output with evidence for H1, H2, H3, H4, EC1, causal use, safety, or PID validity?
